name: Virtual Shibuya Halloween Festival
description:
As Halloween approached, the widely enjoyed holiday in Japan couldn't be normally celebrated in COVID-stricken 2020. So instead, Shibuya and au built a virtual Shibuya City block-by-block to host the largest ever Halloween gathering.

TEST

3x 'The Drum' awards
Cannes Lions 'Innovation' Shortlist
US $20 million in earned media
VR Awards 'Marketing Agency of the Year'
+400,000 avatars created

name: Smart Spokes
description:
Teaming up with tech startup Eyes Japan, Ogilvy Tokyo launched a road safety campaign using Uber Eats delivery bicycles as the medium.

Uber Eats bikes were equipped with a Gyrokinetic LED that turned each wheel into a digital screen that comes to life once in motion, displaying glowing safety messages like 'See Me', 'Give me Space', and 'Care for Me' on the streets of Tokyo.

2x D&AD Graphite Pencils
2x 'The Drum' Awards
Cannes Lions 'Innovation' Shortlist

name: Mom Knows
description:
To launch the new iPhone 16 Pro, T-Mobile teamed up with Apple for a nationwide campaign starring SNL's Marcello Hernandez and his real-life mom, Isabel.

Co-written with Marcello himself, the spot captured the joy of staying connected across generations, Latino style.

name: Copa America 2024
description:
For Copa América 2024, T-Mobile launched a national spot celebrating the gameday rituals fans swear by.

From lucky jerseys to loud group chats, created in collaboration with Samsung, the film also introduced the new Galaxy S24 and S24 Plus.

name: The Big Giver
description:
Apple x T-Mobile 
The Big Giver

New Product Introduction for the launch of the 
brand-new iPhone 16.

Featuring. Marcello HernandezCarrier nationwide launch + feature holiday spot.

name: No Way ft. Kevin Hart
description:
To launch the new iPhone 17, Verizon partnered with Kevin Hart on a national campaign built around a simple joke: what happens when the most recognizable guy in the room isn’t actually the thing people are reacting to.

The film plays off Kevin’s larger-than-life persona and the cultural obsession around every new iPhone release, turning a familiar celebrity moment into a clean, simple punchline for the launch.

name: Talking Boxers
description:
For a holiday campaign, Verizon brought a classic seasonal gift to life in an unexpected way: a pair of talking boxer shorts.

Playing off the familiar tradition of gifting boxers during the holidays, the film imagines the boxers themselves chiming in, commenting on the moment and reacting to the gift with personality and humor. What starts as a simple holiday exchange quickly turns into a playful conversation piece, giving a voice to one of the most iconic (and often joked-about) presents of the season.
